feed.title: SAP Community - API Management
feed.subtitle: API Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP API Management – InvalidApiKeyForGivenResource for some resources in same API Product
entry.content:
Hi i'm looking for some input on an issue i'm having.

I’m seeing inconsistent API key authorization behavior in SAP API Management (BTP / Cloud Foundry).

Setup

  • One API Proxy

  • One API Product (Order Status Tracking)

  • One Application subscribed to the product

  • Authentication via API Key (x-api-key)

  • Same API key used for all calls

I have 4 endpoints on the proxy and product, 2 work 2 don't.

Working:
GET /connect/products/{{globalId}}/orders/{{orderCode}}
GET /connect/users/search
Failing:
GET /connect/users/{{userId}}/orders
GET /connect/users/{{userId}}/orders/{{orderCode}}

I am getting the error from our APIkey policy:
"fault": {
"faultstring": "Invalid ApiKey for given resource",
"detail": {
"errorcode": "oauth.v2.InvalidApiKeyForGivenResource"


  • The API key is valid (works for other endpoints above)

  • All endpoints:

    • Belong to the same API Product

    • Are defined as API Resources

    • Have GET enabled

    • Use the same proxy and base URL

Only the resource path differs

What can cause resource-level API key authorization to fail for some paths but not others within the same API Product?

Is there any known limitation or special handling for:

  • Path parameters ({userId})

  • Resource path matching

  • /users/* vs /products/* paths

entry.title: SAP IS API Management - API Provider setup to S4 demo system test connection 401
entry.content:
Hi - I am getting the following auth error in SAP IS trial, and wondering if anyone has thoughts.

I am setting up an API Provider to S4 abap system (S4 abap system as per It's Trial Time for ABAP in SAP Business Technolog... - SAP Community). My Eclipse ADT connects fine to that S4 abap system - I then used my credentials from Eclipse ADT in my API Provider settings.

API Provider

Eclipse ADT

I did see this similar post, but steps were unsuccessful for me: System is up and reachable. However, the ping chec... - SAP Community

Thanks,

Keith

#SAP Cloud Platform, ABAP environment

#API Management

entry.title: MTLS in API managment
entry.content:
Hi,

 I want to enable the MTLS in API M can you please help with details.

How this can be tested from post if MTLS working fine or not

 

Need suggestions or documents rgearding the same

entry.title: How do you integrate SAP Commerce cloud with S4 HANA API for Synchronous Price calls
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can you help out in sharing the detailed Integration configuration process involved for SAP Commerce cloud to access S4 HANA via API calls.

Taking example of Synchronous Pricing, where API (SAP Sales Order Simulate API for SAP S/4HANA cloud, public edition) is used, there is not much details on integration configuration to be established in Comemrce and HANA side.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_INTEGRATIONS/47ad58c1a27447949aad8addbee46fca/25ed7c0fd8904a38b3bb2001d0de96e1.html

Some of my queries:

1. What needs to be configured for Destination Taget in BO,

2. Is there any Test API's that are available for integration testing purpose

3. Steps for configuring Client certificates in Commerce and HANA.

Also, Is there any SAP blog/learning tutorial that details on the steps invovled for this Integration. 

Thanks,

entry.title: Inconsistencies found in count of records
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently facing an issue with a record count mismatch between our downstream application and SAP. Our extraction is based on the standard CDS view A_BillingDocument, using the timestamp field as the input parameter to pull the data.

Has anyone encountered similar discrepancies when using this CDS view or timestamp-based extraction?
If yes, it would be really helpful if you could share the root cause or the approach you used to resolve it.

Thank you in advance for your guidance!

entry.title: Unable to Activate API Management in SAP Integration Suite – Trial Accoun
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am new to SAP BTP and currently exploring SAP Integration Suite using a BTP trial account.

While trying to activate API Management from Integration Suite, I am getting the following error message during activation:

"There is a temporary problem in provisioning your account, please try again by clicking on Activate. In case the problem persists, please create an incident in the component OPU-API-OD-OPS for further assistance."

Since this is a trial account, I wanted to understand:
- Is API Management supported on SAP BTP trial accounts?
- Is this a known limitation for trial tenants?
- Has anyone faced a similar issue and found a workaround for learning purposes?

I understand that creating an SAP incident may not be possible for trial accounts, so I wanted to check with the community if there are any recommended next steps.

Any guidance or clarification would be really helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Populate the BP Address Comment using API
entry.content:
In S4HANA Public Cloud, I'm trying to create a BP using API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.

However, there is a particular field that I cannot populate due to the field is not available in the API.

This Comment field in the BP Address. 

Can anyone help how to populate this field using API_BUSINESS_PARTNER?

entry.title: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden
entry.content:
Integration Suite and API Management capability is enabled (Settings -> Runtime) on a BTP free trial.

I am able to test APIM Proxy no problem. But I can't subscribe or create a instance:

cf create-service apimanagement-apiportal-trial apiportal-apiaccess apim_apiportal_access_01 \
-c '{"role":"APIPortal.Administrator"}'

or

1. BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions → Create
2. Service: API Management, API portal
3. Plan: apiportal-apiaccess
4. Runtime Environment: Cloud Foundry
5. Space: dev
6. Instance Name: e.g. apim_apiportal_access_01
7. Next → Parameters
8. Paste JSON:

{"role":"APIPortal.Administrator"}

Creation Failed
Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, API portal' with plan 'apiportal-apiaccess'.

Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

entry.title: API Management - GET mTLS Serial Number
entry.content:
Good morning,

We need to get the mTLS certificate serial number via API Management.

A virtual host with mTLS has been created that can be accessed via API Management, following the guide below.

Configuring a Custom Domain for a Virtual Host | SAP Help Portal

The host, thus created, is used in the Host Alias ​​in the APIM

In the policy settings, we have inserted "AssignMessage" to insert the certificate information into the message header.

But I can't find any way to get the certificate serial number.

1)How can I retrieve this information?

In addition, I found the following article,

How to Read Client Certificate Details in an API P... - SAP Community

 where it says:

2) Where should these properties be configured?

Thank you for your attention

entry.title: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-devportal-trial failed with: Forbidden on Azure
entry.content:
Creation Failed
Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, developer portal' with plan 'devportal-apiaccess'.
Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-devportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

On Azure Singapore BTP Integration Suite. Who do we contact to get this broker service fixed?

entry.title: APIProxy revision xx of xxxx_v1 does not exist in environment test organization xxxx
entry.content:
how to resolve this error.

APIProxy revision xx of xxxx_v1 does not exist in environment test organization xxxx

Thank you

entry.title: -306 - El nombre de recurso especificado no se encuentra en el archivo de imagen.
entry.content:
Hola,  tengo el siguiente problema, tras una restauracion de la mv donde tengo montado sap, los desarrollos que tenemos conectados por di api nos arrojan el siguiente error:
-306 - El nombre de recurso especificado no se encuentra en el archivo de imagen.
tambien tenemos problemas usando el data transfer de sap  nos arroja el mismo error, creo que puede ser alguna configuracion del di api que hay que revisar  pero no se que configuracion habria que cambiar.

entry.title: Extending standard API delivered by SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a task. Extending API from the link: https://api.sap.com/api/API_PRODUCT_SRV/overview with fields from tables MARA, MARC i OIB_A08 (oil&gas industry specified).  I think what's the best in SAP Cloud Private Edition. ABAP Extension or using stardard Fiori app "Custom Logic and Fields" ?

Thanks for the aswers.

entry.title: Updating sales order user status via Patch operation
entry.content:
Hello, 

we are trying to update Sales order User status on header level in the context of Sales order CRUD API - API_SALES_ORDER_SRV through Patch operation. However, we are not able to do so. Tried to update through Property "StatusCode", but unfortunately the response is giving Property invalid even though this Property can be found in the metadata of the API. We are trying with the following batch request:

--batch
Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=changeset

--changeset
Content-Type: application/http
Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary
Content-ID: 1

PATCH A_SalesOrder(SalesOrder='XXXXXXXXXX') HTTP/1.1
Accept: application/json
If-Match: *
Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8

{"StatusCode":"E0029"}


--changeset--
--batch--

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Recommended Approach for Publishing CAP Services via Third‑Party API Gateways (WSO2 vs SAP API Manag
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

after checking with SAP Support, I have already confirmed that publishing CAP services through a third‑party API gateway (such as WSO2) is technically supported.

What I still need to clarify, and I hope the community can help, is whether this approach is also recommended, and whether adopting a non‑SAP gateway could mean losing key features normally provided by SAP Integration Suite API Management, which we already have active in our landscape.

Remaining Open Questions

1. Recommendation
Even if supported, does SAP recommend using SAP API Management for external exposure of CAP services, especially from a governance and security point of view?

2. Feature Gaps
If we use a third‑party API gateway, do we risk losing important capabilities such as:

  • OAuth/XSUAA integration and token handling
  • Built‑in security policies provided by SAP API Management
  • Deeper integration with BTP services

3. Best Practices
Is there any official SAP best practice that clearly states when a non‑SAP API gateway is appropriate and when SAP API Management should be preferred?

Any guidance, references, or practical experience would be very helpful.
Thanks!

entry.title: oInventoryGenEntry creation error
entry.content:
SAPbobsCOM.Recordset recordSet = (SAPbobsCOM.Recordset)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);
recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OWOR WHERE Comments like '%{process.UserKey}%'");
if (!recordSet.EoF) // Ellenőrzés, hogy van-e eredmény
{

string docEntry = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();

SAPbobsCOM.ProductionOrders productionOrder =
(SAPbobsCOM.ProductionOrders)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oProductionOrders);

int docEntryInt = int.Parse(docEntry);
bool found = productionOrder.GetByKey(docEntryInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry: {docEntryInt}, Found: {found}");
int productionOrderDocEntry = productionOrder.AbsoluteEntry;
if (process.Output == true)
{
recordSet.DoQuery($"SELECT TOP 1 DocEntry FROM OIGN WHERE Comments like '%{process.UserKey}%'");
if (recordSet.EoF)
{
SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);
SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(process.Material))
{
DateTime StartDate;
if (DateTime.TryParse(process.StartTime, out StartDate))
{
inventoryGenEntry.DocDate = StartDate;
}
inventoryGenEntry.Comments = "dSuite azonosító: " + process.UserKey + "\n" + process.Remarks;
inventoryGenEntry.DocObjectCode = SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.DocumentsOwner = 44;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType = 202;
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = productionOrderDocEntry;
//inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseLine = 0;

inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = process.Quantity;
int result = inventoryGenEntry.Add();
if (result != 0)
{
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorCode()}");
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Késztermék bevételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");
}
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Nem található cikk a jelentéshez");
}
}
else
{
SAPbobsCOM.Documents inventoryGenEntry = (SAPbobsCOM.Documents)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oInventoryGenEntry);
docEntry = recordSet.Fields.Item("DocEntry").Value.ToString();
docEntryInt = int.Parse(docEntry);
found = inventoryGenEntry.GetByKey(docEntryInt);
Console.WriteLine($"DocEntry InventoryGenEntry: {docEntryInt}, Found: {found}");
SAPbobsCOM.Items item = (SAPbobsCOM.Items)company.GetBusinessObject(SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oItems);
if (item.GetByKey(process.Material))
{
if (inventoryGenEntry.GetByKey(docEntryInt)) // A megfelelő sor alapján betöltjük a ProductionOrder objektumot
{
bool lineExists = false;
for (int i = 0; i < inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Count; i++)
{
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.SetCurrentLine(i);

if (inventoryGenEntry.Lines.ItemCode == process.Material.ToString())
{
lineExists = true;
break; // No need to continue checking
}
}

if (!lineExists)
{
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.Quantity = process.Quantity; // Bevett mennyiség

// Kapcsolat a termelési rendeléshez
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseEntry = productionOrderDocEntry; // Termelési rendelés DocEntry
inventoryGenEntry.Lines.BaseType =(int)SAPbobsCOM.BoObjectTypes.oProductionOrders; // Alap dokumentum típusa

int result = inventoryGenEntry.Update();
if (result != 0)
{
Console.WriteLine($"Error: {company.GetLastErrorDescription()}");
}
else
{
Console.WriteLine("Késztermék bevételezés sikeresen rögzítve a termelési rendeléshez!");
}
}
}
}
}
}

The above is my code, and it always throws the same error, the employee is not exist or inactive.
"Error: (59001) [59/1/DocEntry/57294] * Nem létezo vagy inaktív dolgozóra nem lehet rögzíteni bizonylatot!
Error: -1116"
But I checked and there is an employee for my user which is loged in by the DI API, 
I also tried the InventoryGenEntry.DocumentOwner = 44 which is the userid in OUSR, and OHEM table too, but got the same error.

entry.title: Running Hugging Face AI Models with SAPUI5 and CAPM
entry.content:
Hi everyone 👋

After spending several hours researching and experimenting, I finally found a simple way to answer a question that many SAP developers have started asking:

“Is it possible to run AI models inside SAP applications using SAPUI5 and CAPM?”

The short answer is:

   Yes, absolutely!

In this blog, I will share how we can integrate Hugging Face AI models with SAPUI5 and CAPM, and how we can build a simple AI-powered application that can generate text responses and even images.

 

                                                                        Fig 1.1 Hugging Face Models sections

Now the Interesting Part: Can SAPUI5 Use AI?

        When I started this experiment, my first question was:

Can SAPUI5 directly call an AI model?

        The answer is yes, especially for text generation models.

To make this work, we only need three things:

- A Hugging Face account
-  An HF API Token
-  A model endpoint

Once you log in to Hugging Face, you can generate an HF Token from your account settings.

Then you can choose any model depending on your use case. Hugging Face provides model benchmarks and descriptions, so you can easily decide which one is best for:

  • Education
  • Coding
  • Reasoning
  • Chat
  • Content generation

Connecting Hugging Face with SAPUI5

Now let's talk about the implementation.

In the SAPUI5 controller, we can send a request to the Hugging Face API using JavaScript fetch().

The UI simply takes a user prompt, sends it to the AI model, and displays the response.

Example flow:

User enters a prompt:

       What is Hugging Face and SAPUI5?

SAPUI5 sends the prompt to the Hugging Face model.

The model generates a response and returns it.

Then SAPUI5 displays the result directly on the UI.

This makes it very easy to build AI chat assistants inside SAP applications.

 

                                                                                    Fig 1.2 Output- Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

Sample Code:

              Fig 1.3 sapui5 Controller.js - Hugging Face Ai Chat

 

                      Fig 1.3 sapui5 view.xml - Hugging Face Ai Chat

But What About Image Generation?

      This is where things become interesting.

      When I tried running image generation models directly in SAPUI5, I realized something important.

      SAPUI5 works best with text-based AI APIs.

      For image generation models, we need a backend service.

This is where CAPM (Cloud Application Programming Model) becomes very useful.

 

Using CAPM for Image Generation

To generate images, we can integrate fal.ai, which provides inference APIs for advanced image generation models.

Some examples include:

  • black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev
  • Tongyi-MAI / Z-Image-Turbo

These models can generate high-quality AI images from text prompts.

For example:

"Portrait of a futuristic astronaut with glowing blue eyes"

The system sends this prompt to the AI model, and the model generates an image.

 

How CAPM Handles This

Inside the CAPM Node.js service, we create a custom action such as:

     generateImage

This action receives the prompt from SAPUI5.

Then CAPM calls the fal.ai API with that prompt.

The AI model generates the image and returns a URL of the generated image, which we send back to the UI.

This makes CAPM act as a bridge between SAPUI5 and AI models.

 

Why Use fal.ai Instead of Hugging Face Directly?

You might ask:

If we already use Hugging Face, why do we need fal.ai?

   Good question.

Some models listed on Hugging Face are actually hosted through different inference providers, including fal.ai.

For example:

"black-forest-labs / FLUX.1-dev"

This model is often executed through fal.ai inference APIs.

So fal.ai becomes an easy way to run those models with strong GPU performance.

Sample Code:

                   Fig 1.4 Output – CAPM - Hugging face Image generation

 

 

                  Fig 1.5 service.js - first half- Hugging face Image generation

 

                  Fig 1.6 service.js - second half - Hugging face Image generation

 

               Fig 1.7 Fal.ai Api key Generation - Hugging face Image generation

 

Final Result

After connecting everything together, we get a simple AI application:

The application can:

✅Generate AI text responses using SAPUI5
✅Generate AI images using CAPM services
✅Use Hugging Face models
✅Run without training your own model

All of this can be done using free developer APIs, unless you need large-scale GPU workloads.

 

Conclusion

From this experiment, we can see that SAP applications can easily integrate with modern AI platforms.

The architecture becomes very simple:

                                         SAPUI5
                                              ⬇
                                  CAPM Service
                                             ⬇
                    Hugging Face / fal.ai AI Models

 

Using this approach, developers can build powerful applications such as:

  • AI chat assistants inside SAP
  • AI-powered knowledge systems
  • Image generation tools
  • Smart enterprise automation

And the best part is that you can even deploy your own AI model on Hugging Face and use it inside SAP applications.

 

⭐I hope this small experiment helps other developers who are curious about AI + SAP integration.

Happy coding! 🚀

entry.title: SCIM3 PATCH API returning 403 Forbidden in SAP Analytics Cloud (GET works, Admin access available)
entry.content:
Hi folks,

I am facing an issue while using the SCIM3 API in SAP Analytics Cloud to update team members using PATCH operation.

API Details

Endpoint:

PATCH  /api/v1/scim3/Groups/<TEAM_UUID>

Authentication:
  • Authorization Bearer Token
Headers Used

  • Authorization: Bearer <access_token>

  • x-csrf-token: <csrf_token>

  • x-sap-sac-custom-auth: true

Payload
"schemas": [
            "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:PatchOp"
        ],
        "Operations": [
            {
                "op":"add",
                "path":"members",
                "value":[
                    {
                        "value":"d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd",
                        "type":"User",
                        "display":"newuser",
                        "$ref":"/api/v1/scim3/Users/d0bbac78-776890-9876hjmd"
                    }
                ]
            },
            {
                "op": "replace",
            "path": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group:description",
            "value": "patch test"
            }
        ]
Issue

The PATCH request consistently returns:

{
"status": 403,
"error":"Forbidden"
}

Observations

  • GET operations on /api/v1/scim3/Groups are working successfully.
  • Team UUID is retrieved correctly.
  • Team type is userGroup.
  • I have full admin access (BI_Admin role) in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • OAuth Client is configured with API access with App Integration.
  • CSRF Token is fetched and passed correctly.
  • when CSRF token is removed, the API returns 401 Unauthorized.
  • PATCH always returns 403 Forbidden.
Questions
  1. Are there additional permissions required specifically for SCIM3 PATCH operations ?
  2. Does the OAuth client require explicit SCIM provisioning(write) permissions?
  3. Are there any tenant-level restrictions for SCIM3 write operations ?
Attachments
  • PATCH request (headers + payload)

 

  • Response (403 Forbidden)

Any guidance would be helpful

Thanks!

entry.title: How to Rewrite REST-style Paths to OData-style Paths in SAP Integration Suite API Management?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am currently working with SAP Integration Suite – API Management and I am trying to implement a routing transformation where an incoming REST-style resource path (e.g. /customer/12345) should be rewritten into an OData-style entity key format (e.g. /Customer('12345')) before the call is forwarded to the backend system.

✅ What I am trying to achieve

Incoming request:

/customer/12345

Backend should receive:

/customer('12345')

The backend system is configured through an API Provider, which already defines:

  • Hostname
  • Port
  • Base Path
  • Location ID
  • Authentication (Basic Auth)
  • On-Premise connectivity (Cloud Connector)

So ideally, I would like to reuse all these settings and only rewrite the path suffix.

✅ What I have already tried

1. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PreFlow

I attempted to set a new <Path> in the TargetEndpoint:

 
<AssignMessage async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns='http://www.sap.com/apimgmt'>
	<Set>
	    <Verb>GET</Verb>
	    <Path>/customer('12345')</Path>
	</Set>
	<IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>false</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
	<AssignTo createNew="true" transport="http" type="request" />
</AssignMessage>

I have also tested it on the ProxyEndpoint. I have also set the target.copy.pathsuffixto false.

Result:
The backend still receives the original API Provider base path, and the rewritten path is ignored.

2. Using AssignMessage in TargetEndpoint PostFlow

3. Setting target.url dynamically

I tested rewriting the full backend URL in JavaScript or AssignMessage to bypass the API Provider:

 
<AssignVariable>
    <Name>target.url</Name>
    <Value>https://host:port/Customer('12345')</Value>
</AssignVariable>

Result:
This technically rewrites the path, but it breaks the API Provider setup:

  • Basic Authentication is no longer applied
  • Cloud Connector / On-Premise routing does not work
  • Any certificates / trust settings from the API Provider are lost
  • I receive HTTP errors such as ServiceUnavailable or BadGateway

So this option is not suitable.

Only setting a relative path /customer('12345')ignores the suffix.

4. Rewriting request.path or proxy.pathsuffix

Based on Apigee documentation, these would be ideal, but in SAP Integration Suite:

  • request.path is read-only
  • proxy.pathsuffix is read-only
  • target.basepath is read-only
  • Only target.copy.pathsuffix is writable, but it only controls whether the suffix is appended, not what it contains

So these variables cannot be used to rewrite the path.

5. Using servicecallout.requesturi

I learned that this variable only applies to ServiceCallout policies, not to the main backend request.
Therefore it cannot be used for rewriting the TargetEndpoint URI.

✅ Where I am stuck

At this point, I have not found a reliable way to:

  • Rewrite only the resource path (REST → OData),
  • While keeping all API Provider settings intact
  • And without manually setting target.url (which overrides everything)

It seems that SAP API Management does not allow rewriting the backend path when an API Provider is used, unless the path is completely removed.

✅ My question to the community

Is there a supported way in SAP Integration Suite API Management to rewrite only the backend path (e.g. for REST → OData transformation) while still using the API Provider configuration for host, base path, authentication, and connectivity?

Specifically:

  • Where would the rewriting need to happen (ProxyEndpoint vs TargetEndpoint & Pre-Flow vs. Post-Flow)?
  • Is there a recommended approach or best practice for converting REST-style paths to OData paths in API Management?
  • Or is this transformation simply not possible when using API Providers?

Any guidance, examples, or clarification would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Send user login data from SuccessFactors to SAC
entry.content:
Hi,

We need to send user login data from SuccessFactors to SAC that stipulates the last time a user logged in on SuccessFactors.

When checking the OData API Data Dictionary, we only find a field "lastLoginFailedDateTime". We cannot find one for the last login time.

Where can we get these data sets via the OData API?

Thanks
